title: Model Makers, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, milling and engraving machines, and jig borers to make working models of metal or plastic objects. Includes template makers.

tasks.text: Study blueprints, drawings, and sketches to determine material dimensions, required equipment, and operations sequences.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, drill presses, punch presses, or bandsaws, to fabricate prototypes or models.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Program computer numerical control (CNC) machines to fabricate model parts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect and test products to verify conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments or circuit testers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Cut, shape, and form metal parts, using lathes, power saws, snips, power brakes and shears, files, and mallets.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Rework or alter component model or parts as required to ensure that products meet standards.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drill, countersink, and ream holes in parts and assemblies for bolts, screws, and other fasteners, using power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Grind, file, and sand parts to finished dimensions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Devise and construct tools, dies, molds, jigs, and fixtures, or modify existing tools and equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record specifications, production operations, and final dimensions of models for use in establishing operating standards and procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Align, fit, and join parts, using bolts and screws or by welding or gluing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lay out and mark reference points and dimensions on materials, using measuring instruments and drawing or scribing tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Consult and confer with engineering personnel to discuss developmental problems and to recommend product modifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Use computer-aided design (CAD) and computer-aided manufacturing (CAM) software or hardware to fabricate model parts.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assemble mechanical, electrical, and electronic components into models or prototypes, using hand tools, power tools, and fabricating machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Wire and solder electrical and electronic connections and components.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
